_about: Hand-authored map of DATAGLOW's documented capabilities to the js/ code that backs them. Consumed by .github/scripts/capability-drift.mjs (npm run test:capdrift) to keep docs/capability-map.md and README claims honest against shipped code. When you add, remove, or repurpose a js/ module, update this manifest and the capability map in the SAME PR (see AGENTS.md). Each capability lists the file(s) that MUST exist for it to be real, and — for the identity-defining modules — a symbol that must still be exported.
_platforms: Each capability declares a non-empty `platforms` list — a subset of ["browser","desktop","mobile"] — read at runtime by js/app-shell/capability-registry.js to decide which capability modules to dynamically import on the detected runtime (plain browser vs. Tauri desktop shell; mobile is forward-looking and unused today). browser+desktop is the default: the Tauri v1 shell is 'a browser tab in a native window', so anything that works in the browser works there too. A capability is browser-only when it depends on a browser web API the desktop shell deliberately excludes (e.g. the File System Access API — see docs/desktop-shell.md); it is desktop-only when it needs a Tauri/Node native API a plain browser lacks (none today). When a single capability groups files with different reach, an optional `platformsByFile` map overrides `platforms` per file (the capability-level list stays the union of where the capability as a whole is usable).
